After you've bought a Season Pass packag: After you've bought a Season Pass package, it will be valid as long as your OneXOX line. In order to draw data for usage during this period, you just have to subscribe to a Mobile Internet pass. The data will be drawn from your Season Pass and you'll not be charged (again) for that. Therefore, only pay when you buy the Season Pass, don't need to pay again when you draw the data to use later.

OneXOX rates are multilevel. Depends on: OneXOX rates are multilevel. Depends on DA/MA balances .. I can imagine the scenario .. Someone just mnp to onexox, it comes with RM5 credit in MA and starts making a call to an off-net number for 3 minutes and 31 seconds. The MA rate for off-net calls is 12sen/30seconds block. This translates to 8 (30 secs) blocks x 12sen totalling 96 sen. In comparison, the same 3 mins 31 secs call to off-net with DA balance would cost (8 blocks x 9 sen) 72 sen. While an on-net call (with DA balance) for 3 mins 31 secs would be ((4 x 7) + (4 x 8)) = 60 sen. Looking at FnF (limited to 10 numbers) rate, a 3 mins 31 secs call would cost (8 blocks x 6 sen) 48 sen. To be fair, oneXOX MA rate is equivalent to existing prepaid plans from Big 3 telcos of 12sen/30sec block. The Big 3 telcos appears to have standardized the rates at 24sen/minute calls and 12sen per sms. The cheapest prepaid rate to all networks is still Umobile power prepaid at 6sen/30secs block (but coverage may be an issue to some). Second cheapest would be SpeakOUT at 8sen/30secs block. Third would be Umobile older "new" prepaid plan at 10sen/30secs block. All plans mentioned is still available now. Has anyone noticed that Umobile has quietly revised their rates from 5sen/30secs to 6sen/30secs now? .. Let's see how long can they maintain that position before revising upwards again .. I am more positively inclined to onexox ever since they start offering extended validity and season pass. It is a good move and I believe that the next progression would be to just consolidate the DA/MA rates into one very competitive calling/sms rates. Forget about 5sen for 1st 10 secs .. I already tried making under 10 secs calls which fails miserably most of the time.

I recall there is extra charge to "port" the number.
